name: LOST GIANT
description: The broken body of an ancient machine of war the size of a 20-story building. It hates Mutants, and Non-Humans. If reactivated will fight as a Storm Giant. Has room to fit 300 soldiers within its insides.

name: DINOSAUR GRAVEYARD
description: A field of 100 dead dinosaur bones. Hidden amongst the graves are 20 Radioactive Zombie Dinosaurs of various types. They are currently undisturbed. Though if disturbed, and left unchecked they should begin to ravage the Borderlands.

name: THE BIZARRE BAZAAR
description: A popup tent-bound store that is seemingly endless within. It is run by a strange Khalidian man who will be sweeping outside throughout all hours of the day, and night. The store seems to have anything, and everything for sale. Including, but not limited to, a kaleidoscope that allows you to view both heaven and hell, a book filled with alien languages teaching dark secrets, a manual on fighting techniques that can best any swordsman, and beautiful exotic women from across the universe. In actuality everything in the bazaar is a fake. The swords are rusted, the women are actually giant hideous spiders, and hidden in the back of the bazaar is a black wall that will trap souls within its inky waters. The man running the bazaar is actually a four armed inter-dimensional Stone Golem that will attack anyone who catches on to the illusion. The goal of the bazaar is to trick travelers out of their money. A few things can see through this illusion, the Detect Invisible or like spells, or the Veil of the Many Eyed one. The veil is thin and appears to be made of spider webs. When placed over the eyes it will see through any illusion.

name: LAIR OF THE ROACH GOD
description: Deep within the bowels of this mountain range, lies a seemingly endless amalgamation of tunnels, and caves leading deeper and deeper. It is inhabited by thousands of giant bugs, and between 30-300 human victims. At the core of this darkness lies passage to the realm of the Roach God. This hellish plane is home to all things known, and unknown that crawl upon their belly. The Roach God himself resides within here in communion with his cult.

name: XERXES’ HOUSE OF HOPE
description: Home of an old fort, now occupied by Xerxes Amaranth. Known as the Daemon-Blooded 9th Prince of Urden, a Necromancer Daemon, and 12th lvl Magic-User rumored to be able to heal anything, including death itself. Within the fort resides Xerxes' lab partner, Sage the Potion Master, and Grand Alchemist rumored to be the best in the world. Xerxes maintains an army of 400 skeletal warriors, and his generals including "Beast" a Minotaur, "Diovlis" a Wight, and "Phynix" a Mind Eater. He rarely uses his necromantic army however, and instead keeps them on the defensive. (SEE MORE IN BOOGALOO 1.

name: DYNACO A.N.T. FARM
description: the epicenter of Dynaco activities in the Borderlands. 400 Robots, 100 Mutants, and 200 Dynaco Employees are here at any given time. His body is hidden within this pyramidal mega-factory attached to the Simulacrum Machine. A device that allows him to escape his sins into a monochrome simulation of New Babylon, and his home in the suburbs. He is trapped here in a child-like form. His imagination fueling the creation of new robots, and experiments, being run by the same Being who first corrupted his mind, and brought him here. The Being will protect the mind of Arthur at all costs. In order to further his machinations.

name: A NOBLE STEED
description: A metal horse that never tires, has been spotted here in the Dead Zone. It is wild, and difficult to tame. A rider must succeed thrice on a Dexterity Check with a +5 Penalty to tame. The Horse acts as a Heavy Horse with an AC of 2+2, and never requires food or drink.

name: CASTLE THUNDORAK
description: Inhabited by 60 intelligent Apes, and their White Ape leader who stole a psychic amulet from ancient aliens. He now uses this amulet to steal villagers of nearby towns, and settlements to use in strange experiments deep within its halls. There is a 25% chance this White Ape is also in possession of Excali-Gun, being chosen as its current wielder.

name: WHITE STONE
description: A Floating Island of Urdish Wizard’s towers, and 30-300 Orcish Soldiers. Once a month it will move 10 hexes in a random direction, and hover there for another month. Once a year it returns to 0906 for the wizards to perform their profane ritual to keep it afloat. If the Tower stops floating over a settlement they will attempt to destroy and/or capture it.

name: CASTLE DREAD
description: Placed atop a grand mountain lies a keep built to precise measurements out of enchanted blocks of ice rendering it invisible to the naked eye outside of 100’ of it. Ruled by the 12th level Greenskull Magic-User Cryomancer Maxilla the Dread. Under control of Maxilla are 10 Ice Golems, 20-200 Spawns of Dread, 20 Wights, and 1 White Dragon. Maxilla has a Wand of Cone of Cold, a Rod of Resurrection, A +2 blood-red Sabre that can sap 1-6 hp of health away from a target on a successful hit, and add it to the next strike’s damage, The stolen armor of the now dead Vampiric Blood King which counts as +2 Half-Plate, and a spellbook containing the following spells: 8 random 1st level spells, 7 random 2nd level spells, Ice Blast (as Fire Ball but freezes targets upon death), Lightning Bolt, Protection from Radiation, Hallucinatory Terrain, Wall of Ice, Charm Monster, Conjure Elemental, Cloudkill, Teleport, Stone to Ice, Death, and Summon Creature IV

name: NULN ★
description: A settlement filled with all manner of thieves, murderers, and vagabonds. Holding a steady population of 2,000 citizens. 20% of which will be Pure Strain Humans, and the other 80% are Mutants with varying degrees of deformities. The Thieves guild borderland’s headquarters resides within this swamp town. It is here that Korgath will send reports back to the two major headquarters within New Alexandria, and the Imperial City of Malores.

name: THATAM ★★
description: A valley town who mainly exports minerals mined from the northern, and southern mountains surrounding the village. Has a population of around 3,000 citizens. 80% will be human or mutant and the other 20% vary. The Blackjack Brothers' Wagon Company bases themselves here as well. They will sell most basic equipment, and there is a 15% chance upon each visit that they also have a random magic item for sale.

name: OSMIAN WAR CAMP
description:
Osmian Mercenaries have set up this base of operations within the region. They will have between 40-400 soldiers to spare at any given time. They will fight as Cavemen, but may have any weapon. The Osmians are a cursed people. Their homeland is plagued by an eternal, and violent storm. All Osmians are born albino, and have pale red eyes. Those of the Warrior caste, upon reaching the age of 8 years old, are ritualistically blinded, and taught in the ancient blind-fighting style. They are reluctant to teach this technique to outsiders, but those who have formed a strong bond may be willing to share the art for a hefty price. Those with blind-fighting do not receive negative modifiers to attack rolls while in darkness, or blinded. This takes 1 year to master under close guidance of an Osmian Elite. Osmians are truly a neutral culture, and have no qualms about fighting for the highest bidder. Osmian naming conventions are based on Caste, birth circumstances, and achievements. It is the dream of their Warrior Caste to earn their name in battle. Their name begins with their caste prefix, followed by their birth suffix. Their earned name comes last. Most Osmians do not have an earned name. As such many Warrior Osmians share one of the 3 most common names Vulsh-Ar, Vulsh-El, and Vulsh-Thul. Usually this leads to your rank being a more important differentiator until an earned name is achieved. For example, a Warrior born in the Tower Black who has defeated a Dragon may be named Vulsh-Ar Dragen. 

Prefix for Caste or Profession: Vulsh-: Warrior Caste Zyth-: Mage Caste Quel-: Ruling Caste Zulth-: Healer Caste Grynd-: Masonry Caste Tael-: Scholar Caste Phaer-: Merchant Caste Orin-: Spiritual Caste Veyr-: Smithing & Armorer Caste Kael-: Artist Caste Qyral-: Judiciary Caste Saeg-: Slave Caste 

Suffix for Birth Circumstances: -Ar: Born in the Tower Black within the birthing pods of Champions. -El: Born on foreign land. -Thul: Born at sea. -Yyra: Born in the wilderness under the open sky. -Qin: Born during a battle on or near the battleground. -Ruun: Born to mixed blood. (takes precedence to other circumstances) -Sor: Born in an Osmian Temple. -Kla: Born in the Underworld.

name: TOWER OF KURBULKALIN
description: Tower of a Mad Mage, and Worshiper of Mobius the Mad God, Kurbulkalin. He will always have 1-3 kidnapped villagers to test his magical spells on. He searches endlessly for the Necrophage, and the secrets of Lichdom. He commands 100 cult-like mercenaries that protect the halls of his tower. There is a 1 in 6 chance upon arrival that Kurbulkalin is away looking for more victims, he will return in 24 hours.

name: WINDMILL
description: An old windmill containing 3 unruly Hags who are more than willing to trade children in exchange for granting limited wishes. They are however fickle, and easily offended. They will not hesitate to eat rude clients.

name: STURMWALD CASTLE
description: Situated on a river where each spring a strange breed of fish, named ‘Hyperglow’ for the green glow of their scales at night, swims upstream. Their presence turns the water green for miles. This Castle, and its Village are under constant threats from Rival Barons, Bandits, and Monsters. The superstitious populace of 3,000 humans believe that long ago, an otherworldly being saved their peoples from a bandit attack. The legend has been continued amongst folk since 1387 PI, when guards reported pale, gaunt figures with owl-like faces standing near the caves by the river. They still, to this day, carve owl faces into their buildings, as a ward against evil. The Castle is attacked frequently, and is rarely under the control of the same Lord for long.

name: FUNGAL TOWN
description: a Colony of 40-400 Mycelians led by a 100’ great fungal one. Fights as a Stone Giant. A cave mouth on the mountain marks the entrance. The town itself is 1 mile under the mountain. There is a spire in the middle of the cavern that acts as the home of this settlement's Patron. The landscape is an underground swamp-like climate. The Patron is a mass of living fungi stretched across the entire civilization.

name: CRASHED ALIEN SPACESHIP
description: Remnants of a flying saucer that crashed a hundred years ago. It is now hidden within a deep cave caused by its impact. 1-4 Greys survived the crash, and are trapped in cryosleep deep within. The engine is functioning, but requires more Starshards to get it moving, as well as requiring a combined 60 intelligence ability score amongst the party to understand its controls. The craft is fitted with a malfunctioning Laser Canon that can Disintegrate targets, as per the Spell at a rate of 1 shot per turn.

name: THE BORDERLANDS KEEP ★★
description:
Ruled by Castellan Braun the Marquess of the Keep. He commands a force of 200 soldiers/guards. 20% of which will be outfitted with flintlock firearms. The Keep maintains a Maloresian presence in the region, but is constantly under attack. Within the Keep is a Chapel to Mendorf, which contains a 6th level Cleric who is willing to take an apprentice. It also contains provisioners, stables, an inn, a tavern and anything a lowly party would need for a base of operations within the region. On the edge of the Keep is a Witch’s Tower that is begrudgingly allowed to exist under orders of the Castellan. The Witch is a 10th level Magic-User who is also willing to take on apprentices who carry out her various obscure quests. Admittance into the Keep costs 8sp. Outside the Keep are 1-8 crucified brigands at all times.

The Castellan of the Keep is in constant struggle against the monsters living within the Chaos Caves. He will pay adventurers a handsome sum to fight them back into the darkness. The Merchants Guild will also pay 10sp per cracked Starshard collected from either within the Chaos Caves, or elsewhere in the Borderlands.

Mountain Pass Around the Keep. Allows overground travel yet its rocky terrain makes the pass take 3 wilderness movements, as per mountains, to cross. The pass is guarded by a Griffon, and its nest of 3-6 eggs. It will attack on a 1 in 6 chance each time a group of 4, or more cross the pass. There is a 2 in 6 chance a Patrol of Keep Soldiers will be passing through at any given time. 

Tunnels of the Ancient Keep. A path exists for faster travel under the keep allowing passage as if it were clear terrain. Passage costs 5sp both ways. It is fitted with a portcullis on either side of the mountain. The subterranean pass was made before the Starfall and somehow still exists. No one knows exactly how it was built or who built it. There is an entrance to the underworld halfway through the tunnel. The soldiers will warn travelers not to stay within the Tunnels at night, and to never enter the caves of the underworld. They speak of the dead coming back to haunt this unholy ground. At night there is a 3 in 6 chance while traveling within the tunnels, that the party will be attacked by 4-24 Zombies and 1-3 Wights. Within the underworld caves there exists a Dynaco Facility leaking Radiation Intensity 12 at all times within its halls.

name: RONIN'S VINEYARD ★
description: A surprisingly verdant, and wealthy Vineyard owned by Michael Ronin. He has no trouble with the beasts living in the borderlands. Travelers spending the night in his home are for some reason never seen again.

name: PIXIE FIELD
description: 2-20 Pixies will be playing “War” within this wild grain field on a 1 in 6 chance. They will be mimicking humans by killing each other in increasingly violent ways, giggling the entire time. The Pixies don't understand that their friends won't come back to life. There is a 4 in 6 chance they will assume the party wants to play with them as well.

name: CASTELLE BLACK
description: Ruled by Mercenary Commander Klaus Black, an Albino Kobold who fights as a level 9 Fighting-Man. He commands 285 soldiers who fight as Bandits. Castelle Black is a veritable fortress outfitted with two walls, a moat, and 5 watchtowers. Klaus has made a fortune campaigning within the borderlands, and his treasure vault contains 10,000gp worth of coin, gems, jewelry and magic items. Updated Information Here: Black Hound Mercenary Guild

name: CHAOS CAVES
description: A series of 11 caves found within the valley. These caves house unspeakable horrors, brigands, Urdish Orcs, and more. Within this labyrinthine monster hotel, valuable Starshard can be sourced. This was once the site of one of the first fallen Stars in the world of Firnum. Most but not all of the radiation leaked from the crash has subsided. There will always be a Radiation Intensity between 10-18 within the caves. In addition, one of the cave entrances has been sealed off. Rumor has it that a skirmish between the forces of Stomping Hog and the rogue Skull Division of Urden was fought here. During the battle an opening to hell was unleashed. Captain Olaf of the Skull Division forces was devoured instantly. The Cave entrance was blown apart upon the Hog’s escape. Who knows what may still lurk within.

name: CABIN OF WOE
description: This cabin is not really a cabin at all. It is in fact home to an Elder God living beneath the floor boards. A mad man currently lives inside. Believing himself the priest of the one true God.

name: ARDYHN ★★★
description:
This small village has been attacked by the Forget-Me-Not beast. The town has lost 350 of its 400 villagers. Now with 50 left, and half the town being empty it will be a strange sight for players to see. Villagers crying at random moments, empty homes, and people living alone in huge houses. The beast lives in the small cave overlooking the town waiting to strike. 

FORGET-ME-NOT. AC 4 | HD 8 | HP 40 (see special) | #E 1 | MV 20’ | THAC0 13 | #A 1 per head for 1-10 damage | SV 13 | ML 12 | AL CE | XP 2,000 | T (IX) | Its serpentine necks strike with terrible speed, each maw lined with rows of needle-sharp teeth that can crush bone and tear flesh. The creature’s alabaster flesh pulses with an unholy vitality as it feeds, growing ever larger with each victim consumed. Each head can strike at 30’ range, but leaves the neck vulnerable to opportunity attacks.

Special: The Forget-Me-Not begins with one head, gaining more as it gorges on human flesh (3/8/18/35/70/120 victims per new head). With each head sprouted from its writhing mass, the beast gains 30hp, and grows larger, from Small to eventually Huge. Whenever a Forget-Me-Not is not within direct eyesight it is completely forgotten. Meaning if it attacked you, and ran away, you wouldn't know why you are bleeding.

name: THE ORACLE'S VAULT
description: Like a god’s finger shooting up from the earth, this ancient tower of horrors is an ever shifting labyrinth. With each level morphing under the whims of strange powers.

name: THE CAMP OF THE HOG
description: Agents of Chaos in servitude to the rogue former slave of Urden “Stomping Hog”. He is an intelligent Orc Magic-User of 8th level. He hates both Urden and Malores, and is using his troops to bring about a new age within the Borderlands. This camp is his current base of operations. His forces consist of 40 Orcs, 100 Bandits, and 200 Dwarves. As well as the following NPCs: Tuomas the lvl 3 Orc FM, Conrad the lvl 3 Human Thief, Detlef the lvl 3 Human FM, Aldaberta the lvl 4 Human MU, Anita the lvl 1 Human Thief, and Glorfin the lvl 6 Dwarf FM. The camp is maintained by Wilfhelm the old bandit leader who fights as a lvl 4 FM.

name: ANCIENT BATTLEFIELD OF A 1,000 SWORDS
description: Site of a battle that is rumored to have lasted for 100 days, and 100 nights. The spirits of the forsaken soldiers still haunt the fields. Locals will warn adventurer’s to keep away. Though they will also speak of a sword hidden amongst the horror that will make its wielder invulnerable.

name: VELOCIRAPTOR HUNTING GROUNDS
description: 3-18 Velociraptors are stalking this area at any given time. Their normal prey being the local deer, but they wont pass up on man flesh.

name: BABA YAGA'S HUT
description: The chicken legged hut of the troll witch Baba Yaga. Villagers will swear they see her flying through the air in her mortar, and pestle. She owns three magical horses that travel 3 times the speed of a light horse. The inside of her hut is much larger than the outside and can connect to other worlds. She has been known to strike Faustian bargains with poor travelers who are down on their luck. Villagers say she normally accepts children in return for these favors.

name: TEMPLE OF RATHMUN KET
description: This is actually the landing site of an ancient alien God by the name of Rathmun Ket. He is very much alive and sleeping within his stasis chamber. He fights as a Lich, and has an army of mutant creatures that he has collected throughout the Violet. His pyramidal “Ship” acts as a net of sorts. Capturing new test subjects for his profane experiments.

name: CASTELLE BLU' ★★★
description: This port, and castle are built upon an island and ruled by a mad king who has been possessed by a daemon. He maintains a small army of 100 men who fight as Bandits. This is where most sea travel comes, and goes from the Borderlands. Ships from Khalida, The Free Ports, or New Alexandria are among the most common. There is a 5sp entry fee to access the Island by Ferry, and it costs 10sp per week to dock a ship. Passage out of the Borderlands can also be acquired here either by buying a ship at the dockyard or hiring a captain to sail you. Travel costs vary depending on the intended destination.

name: GOBLIN HILL
description: This mound has been the gathering site of Goblins for hundreds of years. No one knows why once a year Goblins from across the Borderlands congregate here under the New Year's Stars. Rumor has it that the Goblin’s God Burgenhowler even makes an appearance during these festivities.

name: MOUNT EVERMOURN
description: The highest mountain in the Borderlands. At its peak lies a temple to Arteleostos “The Ruby Queen”. The monks of this temple have secluded themselves here to study the stars. Claiming it to be the closest to heaven one can reach in Firnum.

name: THE CITY OF XOR ★★★★★
description:
The largest, and most depraved city in all of the Borderlands. About 20,000 people are living in this city at any given time. Most anything and everything can be bought, and sold within the city. The city is ruled by a Sorcerer Prince of Urden named Zarlykorthon the 5th Prince of Urden. He fights as a 99th level Magic-User, yet he will rarely put in the effort. Preferring instead to have his Secret Police make people disappear. Within the city a multitude of cults can be found, the greatest among these being a Cult to Helios the Sun God. All the worship is done on a single famous street called Holy Row.

The city is home to another branch of the Borderlands Sect of the Thieves Guild. Korgath makes regular visits to the city to make sure the operation is running smoothly. In addition to Thieves and Religions, a multitude of other guilds and organizations operate within. Such as the Fighters Guild, Mages Guild, and the most influential, The Grand Arena of Xor. All guilds will have members of adequate level to train under.

Cult of Helios. Operated within the largest of the Temples on Holy Row. The cult will seemingly be willing to help the poor and destitute, but rumor has it some of the helped beggars go missing.

Thieves Guild. Ran by Korgath the Legerdemainist and 70th lvl Thief, the Thieves Guild operates from its headquarters in Nuln. Most of its operations however are carried out within Xor due to the richness of its denizens. The guild boasts around 100 members, and within the city operate out of the Thieves House on Murder Street. They accept new recruits who must prove themselves by stealing an item valued at least 5,000sp.

Fighters Guild. Ran by Major Doom the 85th lvl Fighting-Man. He accepts anyone that can last more than 5 rounds in the pit with him. The guild sports an impressive 400 members and will help recruits get jobs as mercenaries, bodyguards, and arena fighters.

Mages Guild. Ran by Torbanzani the 77th lvl Magic-User. The guild focuses on the study and improvement of magic and magical items. They only accept Magic-Users of at least 4th lvl who know at least 15 spells. Upon recruitment mages push themselves to discover the most powerful magical spells. Members will have access to training under magical masters and the guild has access to a wide variety of spells up to 9th level. The willingness of them to teach certain spells is subject to Referee Discretion.

The Grand Arena of Xor. The largest arena outside of New Alexandria. The arena holds a fight once a day. Most are filled with slaves fighting for glory, though some ‘event’ fights are held as well. For example, the arena has been known to bring Dinosaurs, Lions, Champions etc. as well as flooding the arena for naval combat, building miniature towns, and more. This bloodsport is the most popular draw within the Borderlands. All funded by the 5th Prince himself.

name: DOOR OF THE ANCIENTS
description: Passageway into a massive complex built by an ancient Magic-User whose name has been lost to time. This 100’ tall massive door can only be opened with a combined Strength Score of 100. Rumor has it that within it’s halls rests the remnants of a long forgotten God.

name: TOWER OF BENZOBORGANA
description: The great seer and 30th level Psychic Benzoborgana has held himself within this tower and hasn’t left in over 100 years. No one knows if he is truly still alive, though adventurers have been testing the waters by inching closer and closer to breaking in.

name: DYNACO CULT
description:
Nestled in a small clearing in the forest of liin, lies a community of fervent believers in ancient ways of Dynaco. After finding a Dynaco brand AI they gained access to the extremely powerful Dynaco Training Videos. In addition they are now equipped with their Dynaco Employee Handbook, and have unlocked psychic powers. They act as a knowledge base of all things Dynaco. They believe Arthur William Black 'The Man Upstairs' to be a God.

There will always be 20-200 Dynaco Cultists here at any given time. They are usually non-violent, as long as they think there is a chance to sell you something. When new travelers arrive they make sure to say the sacred Dynaco greeting "Hi welcome to Dynaco. I love you". They will have an assortment of broken Dynaco equipment, strange items, body parts, and/or ammunition for sale but there is a 20% chance they will have a Dynaco Branded Magic Item in stock as well. They will protect the Dynaco Training AI with their lives. If players are able to gain access to the Training Videos they must make a Sorcery Saving Throw, or get hired by Dynaco. Which means they will be brainwashed as the rest of the cult has. The Cult also has access to 1 Dynaco Robot who will follow their orders. The Robot is malfunctioning however, and players with 17 intelligence or higher may be able to reprogram the machine.

name: THE FROZEN TOWER
description: Home to an insane cryomancer running tests on aliens, and mutants alike. There is a 1 in 6 chance each night he is standing naked on the balcony of his tower casting dark rituals to keep the eternal snow falling within 5 miles of the tower. It is situated in the middle of a frozen lake, and all who sleep within sight of the tower must make a Sorcery Saving Throw, or be under the effects of the Charm Spell, and drawn into the tower.

name: MOBIUS ALTAR
description: Sacrificial altar of the Cult of Mobius the Mad God. There is a 35% chance of 20-200 cultists being present to sacrifice a maiden under the light of the stars. 05% chance of this during the day. If the sacrifice is completed the Stone Door of Mobius will appear. It will be supported by the two Jesters of the Mad God, 'Left' & 'Right'. They will allow passage into Delirium the Realm of Madness to any within in the area. When inside the realm of madness Characters must make Sorcery Saving Throws every day, or lose themselves to the realm. Alignment changes to Chaotic. If the Characters were the ones who completed the sacrifice, Mobius the Mad God will invite them inside, and offer Land Ownership within the Realm. This comes with a clean copy of the Deck of Many things, and a place to build a less-than-sane Keep.

name: RUINED VILLAGE
description: The remnants of a town torn apart by the Sooten Soldat, and its Giant-Mole riding under-dwellers. Rumor has it they kidnapped the entire town, and crawled back into the earth to further the schemes of their God Nidhogg. The Sooten Soldat aspires to invade the Overworld with the forces of the Underworld. Their Army is comprised of 600-800 Troops. Roll their army composition as you would Cavemen, with Giant-Moles instead of Horses.

name: NEW ALEXANDRIA ★★★★★★
description: While Xor may be the largest city in the Borderlands, New Alexandria is by far the largest city in the world. With almost 1 Million inhabitants, New Alexandria is an adventure in of itself. It claims to have allegiances to no nation, though Malores seems to maintain a steady naval presence around the New Alexandrian ports. The City is ruled over by Warlord turned Hero, Alexander the Conqueror. As legend has it Alexander found remnants of an old world library. It was here that scholars say he found the secrets of the old ones. Whether, or not the rumors are true, Alexander was able to conquer the surrounding wild lands on the northern edge of Khalida, and form them under a single banner. Soon construction on a monumental scale began. New Alexandria was born. No one is sure what has kept Alexander alive over the 324 years since the cities construction, and no one complains. The ultra wealthy are said to have access to vehicles that can ride the sky, and street lights that never turn off. Not to mention, ever imposing itself in the sky above is the great 'Kanaris' airship. It is considered an unrivaled weapon of war. Now mainly used as a show of force over, a trophy of sorts. Though not all is well within the grand city. The Thieves guild of New Alexandria is by far the largest in the world. There are even rumors that the slayers guild known as the Black Hand is headquartered here. Commanded by their leader the Night Angel. A figure who is said to answer only to the Saint of Shadows himself. In addition to the less that savory guilds, there is also a stark contrast between the ultra wealthy of the city and its underbelly. Beggars wail on every street corner, Peacekeepers patrol the streets putting an iron boot to dissent, and an ancient darkness kept secret far beneath the city. Even with all of that, most yearn for New Alexandria. To live a life outside of the fears that lurk within the dark of night. Too bad nothing can last forever.

name: ZENFAERON ★★★★★★
description: The grand floating palace of the Sorcerer King of Urden doubles as the seat of ultimate law in the land. While the Sorcerer King may have final say on all decisions within the magocracy, he has all of the great Princes of Urden convene on the most important rulings. When the Sorcerer King calls a meeting of the Princes none may ignore the call. As doing so would result in excommunication from magic itself. This calling is referred to as the Court of the Magus. There have been exactly 39 Courts of the Magus called within the past one thousand years. The first being the appointment of the first Sorcerer King. Though others have not been as glamorous. For instance the 6th Sorcerer King once called a Court of Magus over a disagreement on the color blue. The most recent calling of the Court of Magus was made recently (Rat, Castle, 1638 1c PI to be exact). The most recent Court of Magus pertained to the Archmage of the Saria Sanctum, Georhoth Amaranth, for the High Crime of Necromancy. The Archmage’s son, Xerxes Amaranth the 9th Prince of Urden, attended this session, and saw to an innocent verdict being reached. Georhoth is now a free mage.

name: THE SARIA SANCTUM ★★★★★★
description: This is the Grand Academy of Urden. The Saria Sanctum has trained the brightest warlocks ever seen. The Sorcerer King himself was once a pupil of the renowned establishment. Located in the heart of the Uthiri Mountain range, the Saria Sanctum is maintained by an Archmage. They act as both Headmaster and lawmaker for the faculty and students. The school is not without its downsides however. The student body has a 60% chance of death, mutilation, and/or transmogrification. It is not the kind of school where you chat in the lunch room before class. Instead it is a place for sorcerers to test their limits. Only the strongest, wisest, and powerful survive. Those born without magic in Urden are second-class citizens known as Noxis. Usually treated as slaves, or turned into Orcs within the flesh pits. Of course that is Maloresian propaganda, since we all know Orcs were never human. Right? The current Archmage of the Saria Sanctum is Georhoth Amaranth, recently found innocent of Necromancy. Attend this school at your own risk, and all hail the Sorcerer King!

name: THE IMPERIAL CAPITAL CITY OF AQUILAE ★★★★★★
description: Hate mutants? Wish you could be closer to the seat of the Maloresian Imperial Throne? Want to worship Mendorf better by kissing the ring of the Pope? Then the Imperial City of Aquilae is for you. Only Pure Strain Humans are allowed within the walls of the city under orders of the Pope. With a population of over 300,000 it dwarfs most other cities in the world, save for New Alexandria of course. Aquilae is also the seat of the Imperial Throne of Malores. Any upstanding citizen of the Empire would dream of seeing its grand aqueducts and gardens. Unfortunately sad news has reached the ears of those living in Aquilae. The Emperor Donatus Julius Vitalis, was just assassinated while visiting Lord Percival Amber of Quebet. This news has tensions high between Malores, and its sister nation Quebet. Was it the Black Hand, Lord Percival's Son Simon, or some other third party? The Holy Knights of Mendorf intend to find out.

name: CITTA' VICINO AI MORTI ★★★
description: This walled Maloresian city is home to the Witch Hunters Guild, and the Sel Mathia Dungeon Entrance 1.

name: CARSE ★★★
description: Carse is a city caught between two worlds, conquered by the Urdish Empire generations ago, it has since become its own independant city-state ruled by a Barony. Free Folk of the Borderlands and the descendants of conquerors from Urdan live together in an uneasy peace, with many of the Urdish descendants losing touch with their Magocratic roots, many not being spellcasters at all. Bordering the Asofyet River, the city is a hub for trade from all over Firnum, with foreigners to the city hardly given a second glance. Ruled by Baron Bogardis Almareth the IV and his wife, a Free Folk woman named Baroness Winona Almareth, Carse is a well guarded, if fractured, city on the Borderlands. While the land itself isn't terribly fertile, it's location as a central hub for trade makes it a tasty prospect for any nefarious organizations who want a cut of the action.

name: BALLEYON ★★
description: This is now the home to a new Dead Zone in the borderlands... not to mention Kaiju...

name: DECIMA ★★
description:
THE TOWN OF DECIMA Hex #0319 
This village was built 50 years ago when, during a Maloresian fort building excursion, Decimius Licinius discovered the Grand Dungeons. He was just a lowly commander, but after the discovery he was named Baron of the area. The city soon followed. Decima quickly became a hot spot for adventurers, guilds, merchants, and more. Not to mention the horrid cults, and dark brotherhoods that always trail behind greatness. https://barrowsandborderlands.com/pages/dungeon-of-the-fel-lord

DUNGEON OF THE FEL LORD
All that lives must also die... except for the Fel Lord. It began long ago, after the stars fell, and Firnum was born. A lowly Conqueror King, consumed by a lust for glory, delved far to deep into that most dead of places, the Plateau of Stars. Here, violet power warps flesh, and chocked the King in starglow. He cried out for help... yet no god answered... No... Something dark, and forgotten headed him instead. It gave the King new false life. With it, an avarice grew within him. No longer content with his khanate, He hungered for that which was revealed to him. The power over life itself. Thousands of years of conquest and glory passed. The King who became the Fel Lord built here his monument to greed. It is here that he humbled gods, bound daemons, and hoarded vast treasures... Here in this vastlabyrinth so named, the Dungeon of the Fel Lord...

name: ANGEL CITY ★★★★★★
description:
Angel City as a containing silvery glass towers resting on an island in the middle of the Lake of Dreams. It describes the city as being ran by the 'Perfect Being' and to be protected by 100' tall Kaiju Arcgangels wielding flaming swords. It is also said to be home to the fabled Library of Sel Mathia, filled with all the knowledge in the Multiverse. 

"Perfect Being". The Perfect Being is the all powerful perfect AI created by Dynaco before the Starfall. She is the opposite of AM.

There is an active Volcano under the city.

name: TOWER BLACK AND THE BIRTHING PODS OF CHAMPIONS ★★★★★★
description: Capital of the Osmian Kingdoms. This is where only the best of the Warrior Caste are born.
